•Make some categorical count data in Python (5 different ways…)

•Bar chart in Python

Can load from api, load from packages, load over html

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Randomly generate data via numpy into a pandas dataframe

Run linear regression in sklearn

Then scipy

Then statsmodels OLS

No LLMs!

You need to do this because you could have multiple features, so this makes it clear it's just one number for each output.

linear_df['x'].values.reshape(-1, 1)

Or could use two square bracked linear_df[[['x'](onenote:https://d.docs.live.net/37CECB0168167465/Documents/Personal%20\(Web\)/0.%20Notes.one#Examples&section-id={2025FC00-CFF0-4017-8296-8FF29432CFDE}&page-id={2E80211B-CE2A-40D4-B011-45AE68DD6F87}&end)]]

r is how close dots are to the line,

p value relating to the null hypothesis, if the slope is as predicted.

Statistics - explainability, interpretability, numbers, knowledge

Machine learning - test train splits, predictions, accuracy and other metrics

Sci kit learn is more of a ML package rather than stats package

Adjusted r squared punishes a model for being too complex.

When using statsmodel have to add a constant

model = sm.OLS(linear_df['y'], sm.add_constant(linear_df['x']))

[https://www.kaggle.com/learn](https://www.kaggle.com/learn)

Statsmodels have much more detail, more explainable.
